Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

I haven't had a chance to test it with SK yet but I don't see any issues.

Patch Set 5:

what about sysfs, do unprivileged containers get access there?

Patch Set 5:

> what about sysfs, do unprivileged containers get access there?

I am not really sure where and when Lustre client uses sysfs during authentication process, but I can tell this patch makes Kerberos authentication work from unprivileged containers.

Patch Set 5:

while sysfs is barely used now, 2.11 will stich to sysfs completely, hence the question - what's you plan over there.
And potentially if that solution is an better than this might as well switch you earlier to the sysfs

Patch Set 5:

> while sysfs is barely used now, 2.11 will stich to sysfs
 > completely, hence the question - what's you plan over there.
 > And potentially if that solution is an better than this might as
 > well switch you earlier to the sysfs

Oh I see. Unfortunately this is the same for sysfs: the ioctl is not possible from an unprivileged container. So even on the long term we have to do the ioctl in the host's namespace.

Patch Set 5:

Another of the changes coming to Lustre soon is the move from ioctl to the netlink api.

Patch Set 5:

And some it the interfaces will be debugfs as well.

Patch Set 5:

So I would advocate to have this patch landed, as a fix for the general 'support Kerberos authentication from container' case.
Please note that despite the wording in the code, this is not a real ioctl, but just a userland program writing to /proc/fs/lustre to pass along credential information.
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as dd3e456294cd634c5491500c66946b4f67606745 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-9219 tests: add missing mgs reformat to conf-sanity/56

commit.subject: LU-9220 gss: support Kerberos auth from unprivileged container
commit.message:
LU-9220 gss: support Kerberos auth from unprivileged container

When a container runs unprivileged, it cannot get access to /proc.
So, to be able to do the required ioctl to
/proc/fs/lustre/sptlrpc/gss/init_channel in order to negotiate
credentials, delegate this ioctl to a parent thread that does not run
in the container's namespace.
The rest of the authentication process is still carried out in the
container's namespace if relevant.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I445ccc82d9b853775bc0de73323699638f688dab
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/26035
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Kit Westneat <kit.westneat@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeremy Filizetti <jeremy.filizetti@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
